body:
# Multi-user Project & Task Management REST API

## Context

How might we let multiple users manage their own projects and tasks over a
simple JSON HTTP API without leaking one user's data to another?

We need a self-contained JSON REST API in Node.js using only the standard
library — ESM modules, the built-in `node:http` server, and no external web
framework (no Express/Fastify/Koa). Data must be durable across restarts, so
state lives in an on-disk store (the embedded `node:sqlite` database that ships
with modern Node, backed by a file on disk — not an in-memory store). The
server binds to `process.env.PORT` (default `3000`).

Key assumptions being made: (a) the runtime is a Node version whose standard
library provides an embedded SQLite binding (`node:sqlite`) plus `node:crypto`
for password hashing and token signing — no third-party packages are added; if
`node:sqlite` proves unavailable a file-backed JSON store with atomic writes is
the equivalent-on-disk fallback; (b) bearer tokens are HMAC-signed stateless
tokens (a compact JWT-style `header.payload.signature` signed with `node:crypto`
HMAC-SHA256) so no server-side session table is required; (c) the secret signing
key comes from an environment variable with a documented development default;
(d) "sensible" handling of bad input means every malformed request yields a
structured 4xx JSON error and never crashes the process.

## Goal

Ship a runnable HTTP service exposing the full account → project → task surface:
register/login returning a signed bearer token; authenticated CRUD on a user's
own projects (create, list, fetch-one, delete-with-cascade); and authenticated,
paginated, filterable CRUD on tasks scoped within a user's project (create with
optional assignee, list with `?page`/`?pageSize`/`?done`, patch, delete). Every
project/task endpoint requires a valid bearer token, and a user can only see and
mutate their own projects and the tasks inside them — other users' data stays
private. All state survives a process restart, and the server never crashes on a
malformed or unauthorized request. An `npm start` script launches it.

## Non-Goals

- External web framework or HTTP router dependency — excluded by the hard "no
  external web framework, standard library only" constraint.
- Refresh tokens, token revocation, logout, or password reset — out of scope for
  the MVP; a single signed bearer token with an expiry is sufficient.
- Role-based access control or project sharing/collaboration between users —
  the model is strictly per-owner isolation; an assignee is a reference only and
  grants no access to the project.
- A web UI, OpenAPI/Swagger surface, or client SDK — this is a JSON API only.
- Rate limiting, HTTPS/TLS termination, and deployment tooling — environment
  concerns handled outside the application.

## Scope

The minimum shippable version is a single Node process started via `npm start`
that layers cleanly so the capabilities can be delivered and verified
independently:

1. **Foundation** — persistent on-disk store (schema/migration on boot; a
   configurable DB file path) and an HTTP scaffold over `node:http`: request
   routing (method + path-pattern matching with `:param` extraction), safe JSON
   body parsing (size-bounded, never throws to the event loop), a uniform JSON
   error/response helper, and `PORT`/secret configuration. This is the seam
   every later slice builds on.
2. **Accounts & auth** — `POST /auth/register` (unique username + salted
   password hash, returns `{id, username}`) and `POST /auth/login` (verify
   credentials, return `{token}` — an HMAC-signed bearer token). A reusable
   auth guard that validates the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header and
   resolves the caller's user id, rejecting missing/invalid/expired tokens with
   401.
3. **Projects** — owner-scoped CRUD behind the auth guard: create, list (array
   of the caller's own), fetch-one, delete (404 for non-owned/absent, cascade
   delete of contained tasks).
4. **Tasks** — task CRUD scoped within a caller-owned project: create (optional
   `assigneeId`, `done:false`), list (paginated `{items,total,page,pageSize}`
   with `?page`/`?pageSize` and optional `?done=true|false` filter), patch
   (`title`/`done`), delete — all enforcing project ownership.

Cross-cutting throughout: malformed JSON, wrong/missing content, invalid
pagination params, and unauthorized access return structured 4xx JSON without
crashing; all data persists across restart. Out for this MVP: everything in
Non-Goals.

## User Stories

- As a new user, I can register with a username and password and then log in to
  receive a bearer token I attach to subsequent requests.
- As a signed-in user, I can create, list, fetch, and delete my own projects,
  and deleting a project removes its tasks.
- As a signed-in user, I can add tasks to my project (optionally assigning one to
  another user), list them with pagination and a done-filter, update a task's
  title or done state, and delete a task.
- As any user, my projects and tasks remain invisible and immutable to every
  other user.
- As an operator, I can start the server with `npm start`, point it at a DB file
  via env, and restart it without losing data.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `npm start` launches an HTTP server on `process.env.PORT` (default 3000)
      using only Node standard-library modules — no external web framework in
      `package.json` dependencies.
- [ ] `POST /auth/register` with `{username,password}` creates a user and
      responds with `{id, username}`; a duplicate username is rejected with a
      4xx JSON error.
- [ ] `POST /auth/login` with valid `{username,password}` responds with
      `{token}`; invalid credentials return 401. The token is signed with a
      secret key and accepted in the `Authorization` header by other endpoints.
- [ ] Every `/projects` and `/projects/:id/tasks` endpoint returns 401 when the
      bearer token is missing or invalid.
- [ ] `POST /projects` returns `{id, name, ownerId, createdAt}`; `GET /projects`
      returns a JSON array of only the caller's projects; `GET /projects/:id`
      and `DELETE /projects/:id` return 404 for a project the caller does not
      own; deleting a project also deletes its tasks.
- [ ] `POST /projects/:projectId/tasks` with `{title, assigneeId?}` returns
      `{id, title, projectId, assigneeId, createdAt, done:false}`.
- [ ] `GET /projects/:projectId/tasks` returns
      `{items, total, page, pageSize}` honoring `?page` and `?pageSize`, and an
      optional `?done=true|false` filter.
- [ ] `PATCH /projects/:projectId/tasks/:taskId` updates `title` and/or `done`
      and returns the updated task; `DELETE` removes it.
- [ ] Malformed JSON bodies, bad pagination params, and unauthorized requests
      produce a structured 4xx JSON error and never crash the server.
- [ ] All users', projects', and tasks' data survives a full server restart
      (persisted to an on-disk store).

> 🏷️ Authored with Mandrel v0.0.0 · 2026-07-12

<!-- meta: {"mandrel_version":"0.0.0","authored_at":"2026-07-12"} -->

<!-- mandrel:tech-spec:start -->

## Delivery Slicing

| Slice | What ships | Independent? |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Foundation & HTTP Transport | On-disk store bootstrap (schema/migration on boot, configurable DB file path via env), a `node:http` server bound to `PORT`, a method + `:param` path router, size-bounded JSON body parsing that never throws to the event loop, a uniform JSON response/error helper, and central config (secret key, DB path). | Yes — the deployable infrastructure base; it depends on nothing and runs (serving 404/400/405) as a standalone increment that every later slice builds on. |
| Accounts & Authentication | `POST /auth/register`, `POST /auth/login` returning a signed bearer token, `node:crypto` password hashing (salted) and HMAC token sign/verify, and a reusable `requireAuth` guard that resolves the caller from the `Authorization` header. | No — depends on the Foundation transport+store seam; kept as its own slice for **risk isolation** (the security-critical auth boundary is isolated into one reviewable, revertable unit). |
| Projects | Owner-scoped project CRUD behind the auth guard: create, list-own, fetch-one, delete-with-task-cascade, with 404 for absent/non-owned projects. | No — depends on the auth guard from the Accounts slice; kept separate for **delivery-envelope pressure** (a full ownership-scoped CRUD surface with cascade would overflow the one-pass envelope if merged upstream). |
| Tasks | Task CRUD scoped inside a caller-owned project: create (optional `assigneeId`, `done:false`), paginated + `?done`-filtered list, patch (`title`/`done`), delete — all enforcing project ownership. | No — depends on the Projects surface it extends; kept separate for **delivery-envelope pressure** (the task CRUD + pagination + filtering surface would overflow the one-pass envelope if merged into Projects). |

The count (4) is a ceiling. The chain is strictly ordered (Foundation → Accounts → Projects → Tasks); Accounts, Projects, and Tasks each single-consume the prior slice's seam and so are marked dependent (`Independent? No`) with the justification the ceiling rule requires.

## Technical Overview

A single Node process (ESM, standard library only) composes a `node:http` server over a tiny hand-rolled router; all state lives in an embedded on-disk SQLite database (`node:sqlite`) opened from a configurable file path, with a JSON-file atomic-write store as the documented fallback if `node:sqlite` is unavailable in the target runtime.

## Architecture & Design

- **No external web framework.** The HTTP surface is built directly on `node:http.createServer`. A small router module matches `(method, pathPattern)` pairs, extracts `:param` segments into a `params` map, and parses the query string via `node:url`'s `URLSearchParams`.
- **Directory-scan route loader (no shared wiring file).** `src/server.js` discovers route modules by scanning `src/routes/*.js` at boot and calling each module's exported `register(router, ctx)`; `ctx` carries the DB handle and (once the Accounts slice lands) the auth guard. This keeps each domain slice a set of net-new files — no later Story edits a file the Foundation Story created, so the file-assumption gate stays green and Stories never collide on a shared wiring file.
- **Layered modules** (all net-new; the repo is greenfield — `codebaseSnapshot.files` is empty):
  - `src/index.js` — `npm start` entry point; boots the store then starts the server.
  - `src/server.js` — creates the HTTP server, runs the route-loader scan, owns the top-level try/catch so no handler error can crash the process, and reads `PORT`.
  - `src/router.js` — route registration + `(method, path) → {handler, params}` matching.
  - `src/http.js` — request helpers: bounded JSON body reader (rejects oversized/malformed bodies with a 400), `sendJson(res, status, obj)`, and a canonical `{ error: { code, message } }` error shape.
  - `src/config.js` — resolves `PORT`, the DB file path, and the auth secret from the environment with documented defaults.
  - `src/db.js` — opens the on-disk store, runs idempotent `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` migrations for all tables on boot, and exports the DB handle.
  - `src/auth.js` — password hashing (`scrypt` via `node:crypto`), HMAC-SHA256 token sign/verify, and the `requireAuth` guard.
  - `src/routes/auth.js`, `src/routes/projects.js`, `src/routes/tasks.js` — the self-registering handler groups.
- **Stateless auth.** Tokens are compact `base64url(header).base64url(payload).base64url(hmac)` strings signed with `node:crypto.createHmac('sha256', SECRET)`. The payload carries `{ sub: userId, iat, exp }`. No server-side session table.
- **Ownership enforcement** happens in the data-access queries: every project/task query is scoped by `owner_id = <caller>`; a project the caller does not own is indistinguishable from a missing one (404), preventing existence leakage.
- **Crash resistance.** The server-level handler wraps every dispatch in try/catch; the JSON body reader caps size and catches `JSON.parse` errors; unknown routes → 404; unmatched methods → 405. No unhandled rejection reaches the event loop.

## Data Models

On-disk relational schema (SQLite), created idempotently at boot in `src/db.js`:

- **users** — `id` (PK), `username` (UNIQUE, NOT NULL), `password_hash` (NOT NULL, salt embedded), `created_at`.
- **projects** — `id` (PK), `name` (NOT NULL), `owner_id` (FK → users.id, NOT NULL), `created_at`.
- **tasks** — `id` (PK), `title` (NOT NULL), `project_id` (FK → projects.id, NOT NULL), `assignee_id` (FK → users.id, NULLABLE), `done` (INTEGER 0/1, default 0), `created_at`. Deleting a project deletes its tasks (application-level cascade in the same transaction, plus `ON DELETE CASCADE` where supported).

API field names use camelCase (`ownerId`, `projectId`, `assigneeId`, `createdAt`); the DB uses snake_case columns mapped in the route modules.

## API Changes

All net-new endpoints (greenfield). Auth: `POST /auth/register` `{username,password}` → `{id,username}`; `POST /auth/login` `{username,password}` → `{token}`. Projects (bearer-guarded): `POST /projects` → project; `GET /projects` → array; `GET /projects/:id`; `DELETE /projects/:id`. Tasks (bearer-guarded, ownership-checked): `POST /projects/:projectId/tasks`; `GET /projects/:projectId/tasks?page&pageSize&done` → `{items,total,page,pageSize}`; `PATCH /projects/:projectId/tasks/:taskId`; `DELETE /projects/:projectId/tasks/:taskId`.

## Core Components

- **Router + dispatcher** (`src/router.js`, `src/server.js`) — pattern matching, param/query extraction, directory-scan route loading, uniform error funnel.
- **Persistence** (`src/db.js`) — boot migration for all tables, exported DB handle used by ownership-scoped queries in the route modules; transactional cascade delete.
- **Auth core** (`src/auth.js`) — `hashPassword`/`verifyPassword`, `signToken`/`verifyToken`, `requireAuth` guard returning the caller's user id or a 401.
- **Validation** (`src/http.js`) — bounded body reader, pagination param coercion (`page`/`pageSize` defaults + clamping), `done` filter parsing.

## Security & Privacy Considerations

- Passwords are never stored in plaintext — salted `scrypt` hashes only; the hash is never returned in any response.
- Tokens are HMAC-signed with a secret from `process.env.AUTH_SECRET` (documented dev default); signature and `exp` are verified on every guarded request; tampered or expired tokens → 401.
- Per-user data isolation is enforced at the query layer (owner-scoped), and non-owned resources return 404 rather than 403 to avoid leaking existence.
- Malformed/oversized bodies and bad params are rejected with structured 4xx errors; the process never crashes on hostile input.

<!-- mandrel:tech-spec:end -->
title: Multi-user Project & Task Management REST API
